entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop [Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp.] evaluated for its ability to fix atmospheric nitrogen.

entities.name: N2-FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen (N2) into a form usable by plants.

entities.name: 15N ISOTOPE DILUTION TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A method using 15N-labelled fertilizer to measure the proportion of nitrogen derived from the atmosphere.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil used as the field environment for the experiments.

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTER, PATANCHERU, INDIA
entities.description: The geographical location where the field experiments were conducted.

entities.name: NODULE-EATING INSECT
entities.description: An organism that feeds on root nodules, potentially impacting the nitrogen fixation process.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The agricultural practice of growing two or more crops in the same field simultaneously.

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Grain crops used in intercropping with pigeonpea and as subsequent crops to measure residual nitrogen.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A non-nitrogen-fixing cereal crop [Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench] used as a control in the fixation experiments.

entities.name: DIFFERENCE METHOD
entities.description: A calculation approach used to estimate nitrogen fixation by comparing the target crop with a non-fixing control.

entities.name: RESIDUAL EFFECT
entities.description: The benefit of nitrogen fixed by a previous crop that remains available for a succeeding crop.
